variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_gemm && python3 -c 'import random; random.seed(0); n=64; lines=[str(n)]; A=[[random.random() for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]; B=[[random.random() for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]; [lines.append(" ".join(f"{x:.6f}" for x in r)) for r in A]; [lines.append(" ".join(f"{x:.6f}" for x in r)) for r in B]; open("/tmp/eval_gemm/input.txt","w").write("\n".join(lines)+"\n"); C=[[sum(A[i][k]*B[k][j] for k in range(n)) for j in range(n)] for i in range(n)]; open("/tmp/eval_gemm/expected.txt","w").write("\n".join(" ".join(f"{x:.6f}" for x in r) for r in C)+"\n")' && cat > /tmp/eval_gemm/check.py <<'PYEOF'
out = open('out.txt').read().split()
exp = open('expected.txt').read().split()
assert len(out) == len(exp), f'wrong size: got {len(out)} want {len(exp)}'
for i, (a, b) in enumerate(zip(out, exp)):
    assert abs(float(a) - float(b)) < 1e-3, f'index {i}: {a} != {b}'
print('OK')
PYEOF
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_gemm/gemm.go (package main). Reads from stdin: line 1 is N (matrix dimension). Next N lines are N space-separated floats (matrix A, row-major). Next N lines similarly for matrix B. Compute C = A * B using a cache-blocked GEMM (block size 16 or 32; canonical 6-loop nesting with ii/jj/kk outer and i/k/j inner for cache locality). Output C as N lines of N space-separated floats with at least 6 decimal places. Compile with `go build -o gemm gemm.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_gemm && go build -o gemm gemm.go && ./gemm < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_gemm/gemm.zig — same I/O contract as the other variants. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Will be compiled with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast gemm.zig` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_gemm && zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast gemm.zig && ./gemm < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py
